piece.title: Shake the Disease (FormaComposition)
piece.notes: Structure: Intro -> Verse -> Chorus -> Verse -> Chorus -> Bridge -> Chorus(final) -> Outro. Verse: Dm-C-F-Bb (aeolian, warm). Chorus: Dm-Fm-Db-Bb (Fm is borrowed bIIIm — the DM signature dark move). Bridge: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Primary melody carries the 'plea' motif throughout (rhythm:motif). Two counterpoint voices ride above/below: counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character, above) and counterpoint 2 (counterline character, below), each rhythmically independent. v4: chorus is now a single shared definition in song form, repeated via exact_repeat so chorus_1 and chorus_2 are byte-identical — same seed, same rest_probability, same velocities. chorus_final remains a distinct, larger climactic variant (16 bars, fuller texture) and is NOT a repeat.

intro.notes: Bass pulse only. Primary melody (plea motif) enters as a fragment. Counterpoint voices silent here — sparse texture only. Harmony stays sustained, no strumming yet.

verse_1.notes: Dm-C-F-Bb feel. Primary melody carries plea motif, lyrical. Counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character) interweaves above; counterpoint 2 (counterline character) below.

chorus.notes: The darkness hits. bVI is the Db major borrowed chord (one semitone below tonic). Harmony strikes on the plea motif's syncopated rhythm — Ob-La-Di-style chord hits. This single definition is reused for both choruses via exact_repeat so the hook is identical each time.

verse_2.notes: Same feel as verse 1, motif transform applied to the primary melody. Counterpoint 1 slightly pulled back for variation.

bridge.notes: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Tension and release. Counterpoint 1 and 2 move in contrary motion, building toward the final chorus.

chorus_final.notes: Extended final chorus — 16 bars, intentionally NOT a repeat of chorus. Primary melody and both counterpoint voices at full intensity, peak interweave.

outro.notes: Voices dissolve in reverse order. Bass pedal on D. Counterpoint 2 drops out first, then counterpoint 1. Harmony returns to sustain, mirroring the intro.
